title: Access Engineering by McGrawHill Webinar
content:
Access Engineering by McGrawHill Webinar

Dear Students,

Access Engineering by McGrawHill

has been added to our library's electronic resource collection.

A webinar will be held

on

March 6, 2026

, between

10:00 AM and 11:00 AM

, during which the platform will be introduced and its content will be examined in detail. Further details are provided below.”

Access Engineering is an

interactive learning

and

reference platform

specifically designed for both students and faculty members

in the field of engineering.

This platform provides tools to help students apply theoretical knowledge to real-world problems, prepare for exams, and develop engineering solutions. It also enables faculty members to improve educational outcomes by integrating practical resources into their course curricula.

Overview of McGraw Hill's new AccessEngineering!

Webinar Participation Link:

https://mcgrawhill.zoom.us/j/86799494632

Date: 06.03.2026

Time: 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM

Platform Access Link:

https://login.proxy.uskudar.edu.tr/login?url=https://www.accessengineeringlibrary.com/

*You can log in with your institutional email address and password.

Content offered by the platform:

Spreadsheet Calculations:

Used to model engineering calculations, see the effect of variables, and reduce margin of error.

Interactive Charts and Tables:

Provides thousands of interactive charts and downloadable tables for data analysis.

DataVis™:

Allows you to compare material properties and evaluate multiple properties simultaneously.

Case Studies:

It provides real-world examples from biomedical and other engineering fields that are linked to

ABET

learning objectives.

Video Solutions and Guides:

Includes videos that provide step-by-step solutions to engineering problems and solution walkthroughs prepared by professors.

Course Outline and Smart Search:

Content is matched with over 30 common engineering courses and supported by a proprietary search algorithm.

Sources: Advanced engineering textbooks

(+650 full books, +160,000 book chapter)

and fundamental reference texts such

as

guides and

Perry’s Chemical Engineers’ Handbook

.

Fields:

Aerospace, Biomedical, Chemical, Civil, Computer Science, Electrical, Energy, Engineering Management, Environmental, Industrial, Materials and Mechanical Engineering.

Sincerely.
